race.name: Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo
race.tagline: Desert gran fondo linking Abu Dhabi's cities with massive prizes and community spirit

vitals.location: Hazza bin Zayed Stadium, Al Ain to Sheikh Zayed Festival, Al Wathba, Abu Dhabi, UAE

climate.description: Desert climate in late December: mild daytime temps, dry with low precipitation risk.

features: Mostly flat to gently rolling roads with light inclines through desert landscapes and urban stretches

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: Fast desert roads with long straights, gentle rises, and light inclines; fully supported with 5 water stations.
course_description.signature_challenge: 150km UAE inter-city haul with speed-group competition.
suffering_zones: Desert stretches testing endurance in heat.
Final push to Al Wathba finish.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels in Abu Dhabi/Al Ain; race village at Abu Dhabi Cycling Club.
logistics.transport: Start at Hazza bin Zayed Stadium (Al Ain), finish Sheikh Zayed Festival (Al Wathba); 6:30AM start, speed-grouped waves, 28km/h min avg, bus transfers available.

history.origin_story: Launched 2022 by Abu Dhabi Sports Council as part of Bike Abu Dhabi initiative to promote cycling globally.
notable_moments: 2024: Abu Dhabi to Al Ain, 150km, international growth.
2025: Reversed route Al Ain to Abu Dhabi, 1,000+ finishers, Serhii Sydor wins men's overall in 2:57:30.
Ties to 2028 UCI Gran Fondo Worlds hosting.

biased_opinion.summary: A standout road gran fondo in the desert, blending massive prizes, pro-amateur racing, and UAE hospitality into an accessible yet challenging event. Perfect for speed-focused endurance riders seeking value and adventure without gravel or climbs.

final_verdict.one_liner: Prize-laden desert road epic for community cyclists.
final_verdict.should_you_race: The Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo delivers a fast 150km road challenge across UAE deserts, backed by top support and AED 2M prizes. Ideal for speed groups from elites to recreational riders, it's a cornerstone of Abu Dhabi's cycling rise.
